Entered into a definitive agreement to acquire TruBridge, Inc., to strengthen access to rural and community-based healthcare and broaden care enablement capabilities.
Reported net loss of ₹51.4 crore in Q4FY26 (vs net profit of ₹102 crore YoY) due to mark-to-market losses, causing shares to fall more than 5%.
Board recommended a final dividend of ₹40 per equity share for the financial year ended March 31, 2026.
JM Financial maintained 'Add' rating and raised its target price marginally to ₹1,100 (earlier ₹1,050), implying an upside of 12%.
Motilal Oswal Financial Services reiterated 'Buy' rating for a target of ₹1,270, implying an upside of around 29%.
Slips 4% to 52-week low on weak FY27 guidance and AI deflation fears.
Surged over 15% in two sessions after reporting strong Q4FY26 results (revenue up 32% YoY, PAT up 93% YoY, Operating Ebitda up 106%) and surpassing growth commitments.
Board announced a cumulative dividend of ₹13 per share for FY26.
JM Financial maintained 'Add' rating and revised its target price to ₹730 (from ₹580 earlier) due to strong Q4 earnings, raised revenue and EPS estimates.
Surged 9% to a 52-week high after reporting strong Q4FY26 earnings (highest-ever full-year EBITDA of ₹1,006 crore (+19% YoY), PAT of ₹755 crore (+36% YoY); Q4FY26 EBITDA +21%, PAT +34%).

Commenced operations at new 70,000 MTPA Speciality Carbon Black line, positioning it among top five global manufacturers; LFP cathode active material project and anthraquinone/carbazole facility on track.
Weak FY27 revenue growth estimate (1.5%-3.5%) and operating margin guidance (20%-22%) rattled investors despite solid Q4FY26 numbers, causing stock to fall 4.5% and drag down the broader IT index.
Entered into a definitive agreement to acquire TruBridge, Inc., to strengthen access to rural and community-based healthcare and broaden care enablement capabilities.
